task.title: Set the output format for validation reports
task.shortdesc: Use the --format option to choose how markdown-validator presents its report: plain text for terminal reading, JSON for CI integration and scripting, or CSV for spreadsheet analysis.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Choose between text, JSON, and CSV output formats for markdown-validator reports to suit local review, CI integration, or spreadsheet analysis.

step.cmd: To produce human-readable text output (the default), run the validate command without the --format option, or specify --format text.

info.codeblock:
✓ [1] topic-must-be-tutorial ✗ [4] description-must-exist → Add a description field to the front matter.
Score: 5/6 FAILED

step.cmd: To produce JSON output for scripting or CI integration, add --format json.

info.p: The JSON output includes all rule results and metadata:

step.cmd: To produce CSV output for spreadsheet analysis, add --format csv.

info.codeblock: md-validate validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json --format csv --output reports/
info.p: Each row in the CSV file represents one rule evaluation. Columns include the rule ID, name, type, level, and pass/fail status.
info.note: CSV output is most useful when combined with --output to save the file. See Save validation reports to disk.
